mammoth.name: Asian Elephant
mammoth.forText: Closest living relative — Colossal Biosciences already targeting this pair for de-extinction. Genome is well sequenced and deeply annotated.
mammoth.againstText: Cold-adapted gene suite still largely unknown. Significant engineering needed for mammoth-specific traits like fat deposition and fur growth.

mammoth.name: African Forest Elephant
mammoth.forText: More recently validated as a distinct species with its own genome. Forest elephant lineage provides a separate reference point for mammoth reconstruction distinct from the savanna elephant.
mammoth.againstText: Forest specialist adapted to equatorial rainforest — ecologically even further from the mammoth's open periglacial steppe than the savanna elephant.

nfwolf.name: Greenland Wolf
nfwolf.forText: Arctic island wolf subspecies — the closest living ecological analogue to the Newfoundland wolf's insular, high-latitude lifestyle and isolated prey specialisation.
nfwolf.againstText: Greenland's prey base is dominated by musk ox and Arctic hare rather than the caribou and moose the Newfoundland wolf depended on.

nfwolf.name: Grey Wolf
nfwolf.forText: Nearest continental relative. Pack structure and prey specialisation directly analogous to the Newfoundland subspecies. Already proven as a trophic restorer.
nfwolf.againstText: Island isolation shaped a distinct skull morphology and prey preference — not a direct drop-in replacement for the beothucus subspecies.

dodo.name: New Zealand Pigeon
dodo.forText: Among the largest frugivorous pigeons — body mass and island forest ecology closer to the dodo's niche than most Columbidae. Documented disperser of large-seeded native trees.
dodo.againstText: Different ocean basin with no direct evolutionary connection to the Mauritius lineage. Diverged ~18 Mya — extensive genomic reconstruction still required.

dodo.name: Nicobar Pigeon
dodo.forText: Closest living relative of the dodo. Genome provides the best available reference for dodo sequence reconstruction.
dodo.againstText: 20 million years of independent evolution. Very different body plan and size — extensive genomic reconstruction required.

labduck.name: Steller's Eider
labduck.forText: Specialised bill morphology for extracting benthic invertebrates aligns more closely with the Labrador duck's inferred foraging strategy than the common eider.
labduck.againstText: Primarily Pacific distribution — limited geographic overlap with the Labrador duck's Atlantic range. Declining population adds conservation risk to any pairing programme.

labduck.name: Mallard
labduck.forText: Widest geographic range of any Anatidae — present throughout the Labrador duck's entire former Atlantic range. Best-annotated waterfowl genome available.
labduck.againstText: Dabbling duck — completely different foraging strategy from the Labrador duck's deep diving and mollusc specialisation. Occupies a different ecological guild.
